title: Two Sum
descriptionHtml: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may not use the same element twice.

editorial.explanation: For each element x at index i, compute complement = target - x. If complement is already in the hash map, we found our pair. Otherwise store x → i in the map. Single pass, no nested loop needed.
editorial.keyInsight: Instead of searching the array for the complement on each step (O(n²)), store what you've seen so far in a hash map for O(1) lookup.
commonMistakes: Using the same index twice: returning [i, i] when nums[i] == target/2
Nested loop O(n²) brute force — correct but fails time limit on large inputs
Off-by-one: checking complement in seen AFTER inserting the current element, allowing self-match
